channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Tomori in line to start for England against Uruguay
item.description: Fikayo Tomori is in line to start for England in Friday's friendly against Uruguay, with John Stones an injury doubt.

item.title: Does Chukwuemeka show the demise of the 90-minute player?
item.description: Is former Aston Villa and Chelsea midfielder Carney Chukwuemeka, who has made 97 senior appearances across all competitions without playing a full 90 minutes, an exception or becoming the norm?

item.title: What next for Bellamy and Wales after World Cup agony?
item.description: Wales' failure to qualify for the World Cup raises big issues for the team, head coach Craig Bellamy and the FAW's finances.

item.title: The remarkable return of Roy Hodgson aged 78
item.description: Roy Hodgson's idea of celebrating 50 years in management is not to sit back reflecting on his career, but instead to return to the dugout at Bristol City.

item.title: Salah could play for seven more years - Klopp
item.description: Jurgen Klopp says Mohamed Salah could carry on playing until the age of 40 after the forward leaves Liverpool at the end of the season.

item.title: Scotland highs kept me going, says Robertson
item.description: Scotland captain Andy Robertson says playing for his country this season has been important after losing his starting spot at Liverpool.

item.title: Will Uruguay be tough test Tuchel's England need?
item.description: England reached the World Cup with a 100% record but need to be tested - and friendly opponents Uruguay have the quality to do that.

item.title: Managing in an international break - and what to do when your star player does not come home
item.description: Tony Pulis explains why international breaks can be an opportunity as well as a problem for managers, and what he did when one of his players did not return on time.

item.title: Iran protest before friendly remembers victims of deadly school attack
item.description: The Iran men's national football team wore black armbands and held schoolbags as their national anthem played before a match against Nigeria.

item.title: Farage had Ipswich Town meeting invite, BBC told
item.description: The Reform leader was invited, despite the club claiming otherwise, the BBC understands.

item.title: Wales' World Cup dream ends in pain of penalty shootout
item.description: Wales' hopes of reaching the 2026 World Cup are ended by Bosnia-Herzegovina in Cardiff as the away side score a late equaliser before winning on penalties in their play-off semi-final.

item.title: Tonali and Kean end Northern Ireland's World Cup dreams
item.description: Northern Ireland's hopes of reaching the 2026 World Cup are brought to an end, as they're beaten 2-0 by Italy in their World Cup play-off semi-final in Bergamo, as the home side close in on qualifying for their first World Cup in 12 years.

item.title: 'Copying Arsenal set-piece kings can win England World Cup'
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Paul Robinson says set-piece expertise could give Thomas Tuchel's side an X-factor this summer.

item.title: Byfield to keep knocking on door for black bosses
item.description: Walsall head coach Darren Byfield says he will keep "banging the drum" for there to be more black managers in football.

item.title: Leicester lost £71.1m in Premier League relegation season
item.description: Leicester City report a pre-tax loss of £71.1m from their season back in the Premier League in 2024-25.

item.title: Inter Miami name stand after Argentina great Messi
item.description: Argentina great Lionel Messi will have a stand named after him by his current club Inter Miami at their new stadium.

item.title: Quiz: Name England's line-up from 2014 World Cup match v Uruguay
item.description: It's not a game England fans remember too fondly, but can you pick out the players who started the match?

item.title: Why there is more riding on Manchester WSL derby than bragging rights
item.description: The race for the WSL title and European qualification adds extra spice to Saturday's Manchester derby as United host City at Old Trafford.

item.title: What next for Northern Ireland as World Cup wait continues?
item.description: Manager Michael O'Neill says he is positive for the future despite a World Cup play-off defeat by Italy in Bergamo, and questions about his own long-term plans.

item.title: Wales forced to look ahead after World Cup heartache
item.description: Wales are left with no option but to look to the future after their agonising World Cup play-off defeat by Bosnia-Herzegovina.

item.title: Bellamy bewildered by Wales' shootout defeat
item.description: Head coach Craig Bellamy says Wales did all they could to prepare for penalties after their World Cup qualification bid ended with shootout defeat to Bosnia-Herzegovina.

item.title: The 'daredevil child' scouted by an ex-England captain
item.description: The origin story of Manchester United and England's Jess Park, from her grassroots in Brough to being scouted by Steph Houghton.

item.title: O'Neill positive about Northern Ireland future despite Italy defeat
item.description: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill insists the future is bright for his side after they lose their World Cup play-off semi-final against Italy, as they're beaten 2-0 in Bergamo.

item.title: 'Stunning, breathtaking, glorious!' James gives Wales lead against Bosnia
item.description: Dan James scores a stunning long-range effort to give Wales a crucial lead in their World Cup Play-off semi-final against Bosnia-Herzegovina in Cardiff.

item.title: Tonali strike puts Italy ahead against Northern Ireland
item.description: Watch as Newcastle United midfielder Sandro Tonali gives Italy the lead against Northern Ireland in their World Cup play-off semi-final in Bergamo.

item.title: Why 'candyfloss' hamstrings were the making of Notts boss Paterson
item.description: Notts County boss Martin Paterson reflects on how his injury-riddled playing career shaped him as a head coach.

item.title: England superfan set to sell house for World Cup trip
item.description: England superfan Andy Milne says he is selling a house to fund his trip to watch the Three Lions at the World Cup this summer.

item.title: Record revenue & bumper wages - Wrexham's finances analysed
item.description: BBC Sport Wales address the key talking points to emerge following the release of Championship club Wrexham's latest financial accounts.

item.title: Why is Bernabeu hosting Madrid Open tennis stars?
item.description: Real Madrid's Bernabeu stadium will swap its grass pitch for clay courts when it hosts the likes of Carlos Alcaraz during next month's Madrid Open.

item.title: White must clear air with England team-mates - Tuchel
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel says Ben White must "clear the air" with his team-mates after returning to the England squad for the first time since 2022.

item.title: Fenerbahçe to pay for damages after Forest clash
item.description: Fenerbahçe are ordered to to pay for damages caused by its fans during a Europa League tie.

item.title: Hume rues missed chances as Northern Ireland lose to Italy
item.description: Northern Ireland captain Trai Hume speaks to BBC Sport NI's Gavin Andrews after his side lose their play-off semi-final 2-0 to Italy in Bergamo, bringing their hopes of reaching the 2026 World Cup to an end.

item.title: Watch Celtic's McAneny's world record-breaking halfway line goal
item.description: Celtic's Maria McAneny's goal straight from kick-off against Hibernian in December has been confirmed as the fastest in the history of women's football.

item.title: Man Utd come from behind twice but lose first leg to Bayern
item.description: Manchester United fan Pernille Harder scored twice against her childhood club at Old Trafford as Bayern Munich gained a precious first-leg advantage in their Champions League quarter-final tie.

item.title: Barcelona put six past Madrid in quarter-final first leg
item.description: Barcelona took control of their Women's Champions League quarter-final with an impressive, dominant away first-leg victory at Spanish rivals Real Madrid.

item.title: 'AI means you can play the game a million times before kick-off'
item.description: Could data, powered by AI, help bring about a revolution in football? BBC Sport investigates the technological advances that could change the game.

item.title: Bayern dismiss £170m Olise link - Friday's gossip
item.description: Bayern Munich want to keep Michael Olise, despite Liverpool interest and possible bid worth more than £170m, while Manchester United will make a formal approach for Sandro Tonali and Arsenal's Myles Lewis-Skelly is another summer target.

item.title: Gyokeres Sweden's hero as Kosovo dream of first World Cup
item.description: Graham Potter's Sweden are one win from qualifying for the World Cup after Viktor Gyokeres scores a hat-trick in their 3-1 play-off semi-final win over Ukraine.

item.title: Senegal to parade Afcon trophy despite title being stripped
item.description: Senegal's Football Federation boss calls the decision to hand Morocco the Afcon title a "robbery" amid plans to parade the trophy before Saturday's friendly against Peru in Paris.

item.title: Bellamy rues chaos against Bosnia as Wales' World Cup dream ends
item.description: Head coach Craig Bellamy rues the chaos of Wales' World Cup play-off semi-final loss to Bosnia-Herzegovina - but says his team have a bright future.

item.title: Tottenham manager Ho signs new long-term deal
item.description: Tottenham manager Martin Ho has signed a new long-term contract with the Women's Super League club.

item.title: Hodgkinson pokes fun at West Ham amid London 2029 Worlds bid
item.description: Keely Hodgkinson pokes fun at West Ham by claiming Great Britain will win more medals than the Hammers have ever if the 2029 World Championships are held at London Stadium.

item.title: Fabregas, film stars and Disney: How Como are disrupting Italian football
item.description: BBC Sport visits Como to find out how the Italian city's football club, led by coach Cesc Fabregas, is looking to break up the Serie A establishment and gatecrash the Champions League.

item.title: Russo settles thrilling Champions League first leg for Arsenal
item.description: Arsenal beat Chelsea in a thrilling Women's Champions League quarter-final first leg that featured brilliant goals from England internationals Chloe Kelly, Lauren James and Alessia Russo.

item.title: Salah's best goals for Liverpool
item.description: Watch some of the standout goals scored by Mohamed Salah for Liverpool. The Egyptian forward has revealed he will leave the club at the end of the season.

item.title: 'One of best goals I've seen' - James strikes for Chelsea
item.description: Lauren James finds the top corner with a spectacular curling effort for Chelsea against Arsenal in their Women's Champions League quarter-final.

item.title: Jess Park breaks down her best goals this season
item.description: Manchester United winger Jess Park breaks down her best goals this season in the Champions League and Women's Super League.

item.title: The tie is nowhere near done - Russo
item.description: Alessia Russo reflects on her superb goal and talks about her preferred playing role following Arsenal's 3–1 victory over Chelsea in the first leg of the Women's Champions League quarter‑final.

item.title: 'What a stunner!' - Kelly doubles Arsenal's lead against Chelsea
item.description: Chloe Kelly's curling long-range strike puts Arsenal 2-0 up in the first leg of their Women's Champions League quarter-final against Chelsea.

item.title: 'An obvious error' - pundits dispute Chelsea's disallowed goal
item.description: Nikita Parris and Ellen White agree that the referee was hasty in disallowing a goal for Chelsea in the Women's Champions League quarter-final for a supposed push by Veerle Buurman on Arsenal's Laia Codina.

item.title: Bosnia 2-0 Wales - 'The best defeat of my life'
item.description: Former Wales manager Chris Coleman and midfielder Joe Ledley recall the night Wales qualified for Euro 2016, despite a 2-0 defeat in Bosnia-Herzegovina.

item.title: Salah's five-hour bus journey to football training aged 12
item.description: How Mohamed Salah defied the odds and never gave up on his challenging journey from rural Egypt to become a footballer.

item.title: Numbers behind 'the most polluting World Cup'
item.description: BBC Sport analyses the environmental cost of a 48-team World Cup held across North America.

item.title: Is Saka's form becoming a worry for Arsenal?
item.description: Are there worries about how Arsenal's reliance on Bukayo Saka is having an impact on his form?

item.title: 'Like being stabbed in stomach' - Everton's Snoeijs on endometriosis
item.description: Everton forward Katja Snoeijs explains her journey to getting a diagnosis of endometriosis.

item.title: Saudi Pro League, the US or Europe - what are Salah's options?
item.description: Liverpool reporter Aadam Patel analyses what Mohamed Salah's next move might be following the forward's announcement that he is to leave the club this summer.

item.title: Is the three-week Premier League break good for your side?
item.description: The Premier League is on a break for almost three weeks as international football takes centre stage, but will it give your club a chance to reset or disrupt momentum?

item.title: 'It wasn't meant to end like this' - Salah exit tough but inevitable
item.description: Less than a year ago, Mohamed Salah was sitting on a throne inside Anfield after renewing his contract - so why is he leaving?

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: What happens when a manager gets suspended?
item.description: Ever wondered what a touchline ban entails? BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain what punishment managers actually face.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? This week - McCoist v Griffiths
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: Swaggering Japan provide unusual test for Clarke's Scotland
item.description: Scotland will be tested to the full in their World Cup warm-up on Saturday, with Japan having been the first nation to qualify, writes Liam McLeod.

item.title: Two further arrests following Rangers v Celtic fan disorder
item.description: Officers have now made a total of 11 arrests since violent scenes marred the end of the Scottish Cup quarter final at Ibrox on 8 March.

item.title: Aberdeen sign defender Bwomono & sell Heltne Nilsen
item.description: Aberdeen have signed former St Mirren defender Elvis Bwomono, and sold midfielder Sivert Heltne Nilsen to Norwegian second-tier club FK Haugesund.

item.title: Who are the record Champions League goalscorers?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team looks at which players have scored the most goals in the Champions League.

item.title: Why ex-Palace winger Olise is now in Ballon d'Or contention at Bayern
item.description: Michael Olise has been in superb form for Bayern Munich this season, has the French winger put himself in contention for the Ballon d'Or?

item.title: Laporta re-elected Barcelona president for fourth term
item.description: Joan Laporta vows Barcelona will be "unstoppable" as he is re-elected club president for a further five years.

item.title: From Bodo to Brentford - ranking this season's biggest overachievers
item.description: Bodo/Glimt must be the most romantic story in European football in decades but are they the team that has defied the odds the most this season? Here are all 10 contenders...

item.title: Why Real Madrid have 'complete confidence' in teenager Pitarch
item.description: When an 18-year-old makes Real Madrid history in a crucial Champions League tie against Manchester City, it inevitably draws acclaim and attention. BBC Sport takes a look at rising star Thiago Pitarch.

item.title: Cole appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena
item.description: Former Arsenal and Chelsea defender Ashley Cole appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena until the end of the season.

item.title: Arsenal captain Little signs contract extension
item.description: Arsenal captain Kim Little, 35, signs a one-year contract extension with the club.

item.title: Man Utd down but not out - Skinner sure Bayern lead can be overturned
item.description: Manchester United suffered defeat by Bayern Munich in the home leg of their Champions League quarter-final, but manager Marc Skinner refuses to rule them out.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
